_meta.description: Nominal cooling capacity at design conditions per CSA F280-12 Section 6.3.1. Calculates total sensible and latent cooling loads at summer design temperature. Components: envelope heat gain, solar heat gain, internal gains, ventilation heat gain (including latent), and infiltration heat gain. The installed cooling capacity must be between 80% and 125% of this value.
_meta.formPage: F280 Form Page 3 - Nominal Cooling Capacity

inputs.name: climate.temperature.hottest
inputs.description: Outdoor cooling design temperature July 2.5% dry bulb (d_24)

inputs.name: internal.peakCoolingGain
inputs.description: Peak internal gains during cooling (occupants + equipment + lighting)

inputs.name: f280.peakSolarGain
inputs.description: Peak solar heat gain at design conditions

inputs.name: cooling.latentLoadFactor
inputs.description: Latent load multiplier from psychrometric calculations

output.note: Total nominal cooling capacity (sensible + latent) at design conditions

preconditions.description: Outdoor design temp must exceed indoor setpoint for cooling load

behavior.note: Sum of all sensible gains multiplied by latent load factor for total cooling load
No cooling required when outdoor temp does not exceed setpoint

formula.symbolic: Q_cool = (Q_env + Q_solar + Q_int + Q_vent + Q_inf) × LLF

where.Q_solar: Peak solar heat gain (W) from f280.peakSolarGain
where.LLF: Latent load factor from psychrometric calculations

tests.name: Typical house with cooling, Toronto summer

references.source: CSA F280-12 Section 6.3.1 - Nominal Cooling Capacity
